title: My Grief Has No Expiry Date
subtitle: An affirmation for grief that has gone on longer than the world expects
excerpt: A short affirmation for grief that is still present years after the loss. For the pressure to be over it by now, to have moved on, to stop bringing it up.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you feel external or internal pressure to be done grieving, particularly on days when loss arrives with unexpected force.

content.text: A short affirmation for grief that continues. There is a social expectation that grief has a timeline: a period of acute loss, followed by recovery, followed by the return to ordinary function and the end of visible mourning. This expectation is not calibrated to how grief actually works. This affirmation is for the grief that has not received the memo.
Say it slowly, hand on your chest, three times:
My grief has no expiry date. I am allowed to carry it for as long as it asks to be carried.
The first sentence removes the deadline. The second removes the shame. Grief carried for a long time is not grief that has failed to end. It is grief for a loss that was worth grieving.
